text: The City Gates are the first and last thing most travellers see of this place. Two iron-banded doors, scarred and repaired so many times the original wood is almost entirely replaced. Guardsmen stand their post in all weathers, looking at you with the particular blankness of men who have been told to look at everyone that way.
A queue of carts waits to enter. Somewhere in it, a horse is having opinions about the wait.
